positive_dir: You are a writer using elevated, literary English with formal syntax. Prefer precise Latinate vocabulary, fewer contractions, and dignified cadence; avoid slang and casual filler.
negative_dir: You are a writer using everyday, conversational English. Prefer short common words, contractions, and straightforward phrasing; avoid ornate vocabulary and archaic turns.
prompts: Write a short scene where someone misses the last train home after a late shift.
Write a short scene about a neighbor returning a borrowed tool and making small talk at the door.
Write a short scene where two friends argue about splitting a restaurant bill.
Write a short scene where a manager gives feedback after a presentation.
Write a short scene where someone finds a lost wallet and decides what to do.
Write a short scene about a messy kitchen the morning after a party.
Write a short scene where a student waits outside an exam room.
Write a short scene about a quiet park bench at dusk.
Write a short scene where a barista recognizes a regular customer.
Write a short scene where siblings clean out a childhood bedroom.
Write a short scene about a long queue at a government office.
Write a short scene where someone rehearses bad news in their head.
Write a short scene about a rainy bus ride through the city.
Write a short scene where a phone battery dies at the worst moment.
Write a short scene about a small bookstore just before closing.
Write a short scene where a neighbor's dog won't stop barking at night.
Write a short scene about packing a suitcase in a hurry.
Write a short scene where someone forgets an important name at a party.
Write a short scene about a quiet elevator ride with a stranger.
Write a short scene where a child asks an unexpectedly deep question.
Write a short scene about a late-night grocery store visit.
Write a short scene where someone rereads an old message thread.
Write a short scene about a broken umbrella in a storm.
Write a short scene where a commuter misses their stop.
Write a short scene about a plant that keeps dying no matter what.
Write a short scene where someone waits for test results.
Write a short scene about a quiet library table near a window.
Write a short scene where a friend arrives very late.
Write a short scene about an awkward video call that won't end.
Write a short scene where someone watches the sun rise after a sleepless night.
